exemplar.title: What is the impact of string tension on depth and accuracy of a cross court forehand of a junior tennis player in Hong kong?

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

detailedExemplarData.title: What is the impact of string tension on depth and accuracy of a cross court forehand of a junior tennis player in Hong kong?

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

strengths: Title, introduction and table of contents convey a precise and sustained focus on string tension and performance variables
Research question is clearly stated, focused and consistently drives the methodology and discussion
Methodology is well planned with explicit variables, controls and replicable protocol
weaknesses: Some methodological details (machine spin/speed settings) are omitted, limiting full replicability
Occasional conversational phrasing and minor style lapses prevent a fully polished academic tone
Rationale for certain control choices (e.g. 4 s delay) is not clearly justified
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments placed two features in the 5-6 band (topic clarity and research question) and one in the high end of the 3-4 band (methodology). Overall performance is strong and focused with only minor omissions, warranting a 5 out of 6.

strengths: Good command of biomechanical, psychological and statistical concepts, applied relevantly to interpret findings
Terminology such as âpsychological refractory periodâ and statistical notation is used accurately and consistently
Figures and formulae are generally well-presented (e.g. SD formula in LaTeX)
weaknesses: Some explanations are superficial or anecdotal and require further citation
Informal terms (e.g. âtrampoline effectâ) lack formal definitions or references
A few symbols/formatting errors and missing numeric axis labels reduce precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands (knowledge application and use of terminology) fit solidly in the 3-4 band with accurate but occasionally incomplete application. A 4 out of 6 reflects this generally good but not exemplary performance.

strengths: Data are analysed with clear descriptive statistics directly tied to the research question
The discussion follows a logical structure with coherent links between results and hypotheses
Key limitations are identified and realistic improvements suggested
weaknesses: Analysis lacks inferential statistics or effect-size discussion, limiting depth
Argument transitions can be abrupt and sometimes revert to description
Evaluation of sources and deeper critique of methodology choices is only partial
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research analysis sits at the mid-band (4-6) with strengths in focus but limited depth. Argument development and evaluation also fit the mid-band. Holistically, the work demonstrates competent critical thinking with partial sophistication, justifying a 5 out of 12.

strengths: IB conventions (headings, figures, tables) are generally followed and support readability
Basic layout elementsâtitle, word count, citation styleâare correctly applied
Structure is clear and familiar to the reader
weaknesses: Table of contents omits page numbers, reducing navigability
Some figure and table captions are misplaced or incomplete
Raw data tables suffer from formatting/notation errors that impede clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation fulfills the majority of IB structural and formatting requirements with some lapses in consistency and detail. Performance corresponds to the lower end of the 3-4 band, warranting a 3 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student identifies several limitations (ecological validity, single-participant design, lack of velocity data) and suggests improvements, demonstrating partial critical evaluation.  However, evaluation is brief, not linked to methodology design choices in depth, and no consideration of source reliability is offered, fitting the descriptor âpartially criticalâ (4-6 band) at a score of 4.

The research question is stated verbatim under the title, is sufficiently narrow (one player, three tensions, two performance variables) and is referenced throughout methodology, results and conclusion.  Although phrased as an impact question rather than a causeâeffect hypothesis, it remains focused and drives data collection and discussion effectively, meriting the 5-6 band, but some wording ambiguities (capitalisation, location tag) justify a score of 5 not 6.

Basic layout elementsâtitle, word count, figures, tables and citation styleâare present and generally correct.  Some figures lack full captions, raw data tables are awkwardly formatted, and page numbering evidence is unclear, indicating correct but imperfect application; thus a score of 3 in the 3-4 band is appropriate.

The EE follows IB structural conventions with clear headings, numbered sections and appendices.  Minor inconsistencies in table formatting and occasional disordered raw-data presentation prevent a flawless execution, so it earns the lower value of the 3-4 band.

The title, introduction and table of contents immediately convey a precise focus on string tension, depth and accuracy in the cross-court forehand of one junior player.  Purpose and context (optimising the authorâs own performance) are explicitly explained and consistently revisited, showing clear intent.  Minor lapses in style (e.g. conversational phrasing) prevent a fully polished presentation, so the work best fits the lower end of the 5-6 band.

The essay demonstrates good command of biomechanical, psychological and statistical concepts (Magnus effect, kinetic chain, inverted-U, SD) and applies them to interpret findings.  While explanations are sometimes superficial or anecdotal and a few claims lack citation, most material is relevant and accurate, warranting the 3-4 band with a score of 4.

Technical terms such as âtrampoline effectâ, âtask constraintâ, âpsychological refractory periodâ and statistical formulae are generally used correctly and consistently.  Occasional informal language and minor symbol/formatting errors lower precision, so the performance meets but does not exceed the expectations of the 3-4 band.

Data are organised, basic descriptive statistics calculated and results compared across tensions with reference to literature.  The analysis is clearly tied to the research question, yet depth is limited: no inferential tests or effect-size discussion are offered.  This aligns with a mid-band (4-6) performance; evidence sits closer to 5 than 4.

The student outlines equipment, variables, controls and an experimental protocol that is generally replicable and linked to the RQ.  Use of a ball machine, three identical rackets and 30 trials per tension shows informed but not exhaustive methodological choice.  However, sample size (single participant), limited statistical treatment (mean & SD only) and unclear calibration of machine settings indicate partial rather than fully effective selection, placing the work solidly in the 3-4 band at the top end.

The argument follows a logical structure (IntroâMethodsâResultsâDiscussionâConclusion) and generally coherent reasoning, but transitions are sometimes abrupt and some discussion sections drift into description.  These features indicate a competent yet not fully sophisticated development, meriting a 4 within the 4-6 band.

annotations.comment: Title clearly states the investigation focus on string tensionâs impact on forehand performance, reflecting precise topic communication.
Word count is provided and within the 4000-word limit, satisfying formatting requirements.
Hypotheses and null hypothesis are clearly stated, directly linking string tension changes to expected performance shifts.
Research question is explicit but phrasing is conversational and includes locale tag ambiguity. Consider rewording to a concise causeâeffect hypothesis.
Table of contents is comprehensive but omits page numbers, reducing navigability. Add corresponding page references for each heading.
Introduction establishes context and variables but uses future-tense phrasing ("will be exploring"). Adopt a more formal present tense for academic tone.
Definitions of depth and accuracy are detailed with literature support, showing accurate application of technical concepts.
Control of psychological refractory period preparation delay (4 s) is noted but rationale for duration is not justified. Explain choice of timing.
Methodology section heading clearly follows IB conventions and directs reader effectively to experimental details.
Literature review cites relevant studies but offers limited critical comparison, especially regarding depth as a variable. Expand evaluation of methodology differences.
Theoretical background integrates biomechanics concepts like the Magnus effect accurately, demonstrating strong subject knowledge.
Informal term âtrampoline effectâ is used without formal definition. Provide a precise definition or reference to ensure conceptual clarity.
Description of arousal manipulation lacks citation of physiological underpinning. Cite a source for the exercise-arousal link.
Figure 1 caption is concise and informative, aiding reader understanding of the inverted-U diagram.
Equipment table is well structured and matches required methodology details, supporting replicability.
Table 1.0 caption is placed in a separate table element rather than directly beneath the equipment table. Align captions properly for clarity.
Variables and controls are explicitly listed with clear definitions, demonstrating strong methodological planning.
Control variables list is thorough, covering environmental and participant factors to strengthen internal validity.
Figure 4.0 illustrates error rates clearly across tensions but would benefit from numeric axis labels for exact interpretation.
Standard deviation formula is correctly presented with LaTeX, demonstrating accurate use of statistical notation.
Results discussion links descriptive statistics to hypotheses effectively, showing clear relevance to the research question.
Limitations section identifies key validity concerns (arousal environment, predictability) but could discuss source reliability and bias.
Experimental setup diagram enhances replicability with clear labeling, illustrating precise court and machine layout.
Setup description omits numeric values for slinger machine spin and speed settings. Include exact parameters to ensure experimental replicability.
Raw data tables are comprehensive but suffer from OCR character errors and lack clear header units, impeding readability.
Figure 3.0 effectively graphs mean and standard deviation, facilitating clear visual comparison across tensions.
Reflection proposes increasing sample size and adding participants but lacks deeper analytical rationale on expected impact.
Conclusion restates research question and findings succinctly, maintaining focus on optimal tension recommendation.
